candidates.foreshadow_summary: Johnsy fixates on the ivy leaves outside her window, counting them down and believing her life will end when the last leaf falls.
candidates.payoff_summary: After Behrman's death, it is revealed that he painted a convincing last leaf on the wall during the storm, which stayed, giving Johnsy the will to live.

proposed_trigger.description: Behrman, after hearing Johnsy's fantasy and seeing the nearly bare vine during the storm, secretly paints a leaf on the wall overnight.
observable_conditions: Behrman and Sue peer out the window fearfully at the ivy vine (P0036)
Cold rain and snow are falling (P0036)
The next morning one leaf remains (P0040)
Lantern, ladder, brushes, and palette are found (P0055)

candidates.relation_description: The leaf that Johnsy counts down is not a real leaf but Behrman's painting, making his earlier talk of a masterpiece ironic and tragic.
candidates.stage1_rationale: The ivy leaf is a central object introduced early with mysterious properties (why does it seem to control her fate?), and the payoff explains its true nature, satisfying a significant narrative debt.

candidates.foreshadow_summary: Behrman is portrayed as a failed artist who has talked for years about painting a masterpiece but never started, with a blank canvas in his room.
candidates.payoff_summary: The painted last leaf is called Behrman's masterpiece, finally created in a selfless act that ends his life.

proposed_trigger.description: Behrman learns of Johnsy's conviction about the last leaf and decides to act, painting the leaf during the stormy night.
observable_conditions: Sue tells Behrman about Johnsy's fancy (P0031)
Behrman's emotional reaction and agreement to pose (P0032-P0035)

candidates.relation_description: The early emphasis on Behrman's unrealized masterpiece is fulfilled by his final, sacrificial painting, transforming a running joke into a tragic heroism.
candidates.stage1_rationale: While the masterpiece motif is clearly set up, its connection to the leaf might be seen as a thematic echo rather than a direct cause-effect; however, it is a strong delayed setup-payoff pair.

candidates.foreshadow_summary: The view from Johnsy's window is a bare yard and the blank side of a brick house, with an old ivy vine climbing half way up, mostly bare due to autumn.
candidates.payoff_summary: The painted last leaf is on the brick wall, taking advantage of the blank wall as a canvas, and its realistic attachment to the vine branch explains why Johnsy never suspected it was painted.

proposed_trigger.description: Behrman uses the blank brick wall as his canvas on the stormy night to paint the leaf.
observable_conditions: The wall is described as blank (P0018)
The leaf is seen clinging high up (P0040)

candidates.relation_description: The spatial description of the window and wall sets the stage for Behrman to execute his painting, making the payoff spatially plausible.
candidates.stage1_rationale: While the blank wall is a necessary condition, it is a very subtle setup and could be considered merely descriptive rather than a deliberate foreshadowing element. However, for a high-recall step, it qualifies.
